tweets.text:
Resharing this ML Street Talk episode with @jeremyphoward again, because I think you should watch it. So many grounded thoughts cutting through the hype and breathlessness.

Also discusses juniors in the age of agentic coding.

https://t.co/RCqANPOVZ5

@jeremyphoward i think how we help juniors to grow in this new environment will be one of the biggest questions to solve in software engineering. i think in a broader sense, we'll also need to solve this in education in general.

which is harder, because education is slow to react and change.
@jeremyphoward overall just love his thoughts on human/machine relation. just ... healthy, for a lack of a better word.

RT @sophiamyang: Hiring hiring hiring! 

We @MistralAI are hiring developer advocates on my team. Join us! https://t.co/J8Vd8Ithb6
seconded.  @jeremyphoward is a true gem. always worth listening to.

RT @matteocollina: Every Node.js developer has lost a background job to a server restart.

We just open-sourced @platformatic/job-queue, a…
RT @sqs: Built with Amp’s new plugin API: https://t.co/wkpV1V59lw
RT @raysan5: It's been 5 days since 0 Issues/PRs. I took 2 days off... kind of... During that time I closed 4 new Issues and 13 new PRs. At…

neat idea! i do worry that it will filter by seniority a lot, so newcomers have a harder time. i also would not like these kinds of scoreboards to be posted on an issue. feels kinda mean. bit it could be one of many signals to make triage easier.
